policies.name: Term Life Insurance
policies.description: Term life insurance provides coverage for a specific period (term), typically 10, 20, or 30 years. It's the most affordable type of life insurance.
eligibility: Must pass medical exam (for most policies)

policies.name: Whole Life Insurance
policies.description: Whole life insurance provides lifetime coverage with a cash value component that grows over time.

policies.name: Universal Life Insurance
policies.description: Universal life insurance offers flexible premiums and death benefits with a cash value component.

process: 1. Notify the insurance company of the death
2. Submit required documentation (death certificate, policy documents)
common_reasons_for_denial: Policy not in force at time of death

faq.what_is_term_life: Term life insurance provides coverage for a specific period. If the insured dies during the term, beneficiaries receive the death benefit.
faq.how_much_coverage: A common rule is to get coverage equal to 10-15 times your annual income, but individual needs vary.
faq.what_affects_premium: Age, health status, lifestyle (smoking), occupation, coverage amount, and term length all affect premiums.
faq.can_i_change_policy: Many policies allow conversion from term to permanent coverage. Some allow policy upgrades.
faq.what_is_waiting_period: The contestability period is typically 2 years after policy issue. During this time, insurers can deny claims for misrepresentation.
